title: Viewing Party

jokes.text: Just say copies. Why do you have to drag cats into this?
jokes.setup: Someone uses the term 'copycats' in context of crime reporting
jokes.punchline: Jim takes the compound word literally and objects to involving cats
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim-style literal interpretation of common phrase. Sharp economy and perfect character voice.

jokes.text: It's pronounced 'wacko.'
jokes.setup: Character mentions 'What if it's another Waco?' referring to the historical standoff
jokes.punchline: Another character 'corrects' the pronunciation to 'wacko' (crazy person)
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation joke. Gets a chuckle but not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: I bet he's wishing he was strangling someone.
jokes.setup: Andy suggests the Scranton Strangler wishes he had a hybrid car for better mileage
jokes.punchline: Dark response that he probably wishes he was doing what he's known for
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion of Andy's innocent car comment. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: Grandpa, where were you the day the Scranton Strangler was caught? Well, I was there, kiddo. I was there.
jokes.setup: Michael imagines a future conversation with his grandchildren about this historic moment
jokes.punchline: He dramatically claims he 'was there' when he was just watching TV at work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael self-importance. He elevates watching TV at work to being a witness to history.

jokes.text: you go sell these and buy yourself a nice spaceship.
jokes.setup: Michael continues his fantasy grandpa voice
jokes.punchline: Gives absurd future advice about buying spaceships
jokes.explanation: Random = funny approach. Mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: The invalid.
jokes.setup: Erin asks Michael who his favorite Glee character is
jokes.punchline: Michael refers to the disabled character as 'the invalid' using outdated/insensitive language
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate language. Perfectly in character and economically delivered.

jokes.text: Take Gabe, take Michael, you make 'Gaymike.'
jokes.setup: Erin explains how Glee mash-ups work by combining two different things
jokes.punchline: Combines Gabe + Michael = 'Gaymike' which sounds like a slur
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocent wordplay creates unintended implications. Good character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Of all feelings, to base a show around glee? Thirst. Now that's a show I'd watch.
jokes.setup: Angela dismisses the concept of a show based on 'glee'
jokes.punchline: Suggests 'Thirst' would be a better show concept, revealing her priorities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Angela line. Perfectly captures her repressed sexuality and judgmental nature in one economical joke.

jokes.text: Tonight might be a convenient night for us to have some intercourse.
jokes.setup: Angela is speaking to someone (presumably Dwight)
jokes.punchline: Uses clinical, unsexy language to proposition sex
jokes.explanation: Angela's repressed personality makes even sex sound like a business transaction. Great character work.

jokes.text: No, he's not the boss. Why did you just say he was the boss? 'Cause you're the boss.
jokes.setup: Kevin calls Gabe the boss, Michael corrects him
jokes.punchline: Kevin's circular logic reveals his confusion while trying to placate Michael
jokes.explanation: Kevin's simple-minded response to awkward situation. Solid character beat but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I just have a different lifestyle. They have these bags of vegetables that steam right inside their own bag. So, I'll get a glass of sangria, sit down in front of the TV, my bag of vegetables... Before you know it, I'm ready for bed.
jokes.setup: Michael contrasts his lifestyle with Gabe's elaborate cooking
jokes.punchline: Describes his sad routine of frozen vegetables, sangria, and TV as a 'lifestyle'
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt to make his pathetic routine sound sophisticated. Good character development through humor.

jokes.text: Imagine one instant of a song expanded to be the size of the universe. I can't even do that.
jokes.setup: Gabe pretentiously describes his music philosophy
jokes.punchline: Kevin's literal, deadpan response that he can't imagine it
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Gabe's pretension and Kevin's honest simplicity. Good character pairing.

jokes.text: And if it doesn't stop soon, I am gonna be up all night.
jokes.setup: Pam explains that her baby's reverse cycling means she's up all day and all night
jokes.punchline: Says if it doesn't stop she'll be up all night (when she already said she's up all night)
jokes.explanation: Sleep-deprived logic error from exhausted new mom. Relatable but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Which one's Glee?
jokes.setup: Michael is watching Glee and characters are being identified
jokes.punchline: Michael asks which character IS Glee, not understanding it's the show title
jokes.explanation: Michael's confusion about show title vs character name. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Turn it up to 11. Come on, Michael. Spinal cord.
jokes.setup: Michael references the famous Spinal Tap 'turn it up to 11' line
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'Spinal Tap' with 'Spinal cord'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism of pop culture reference. Gets the job done but predictable.

jokes.text: Pam, I have a loaded gun in my desk at work. And if I ever start acting like that weenie, Gabe, I want you to take that gun, and I want you to shoot me, like, 100 times in the groin till I'm dead, okay?
jokes.setup: Michael is frustrated with Gabe's behavior at the party
jokes.punchline: Escalates to elaborate suicide instructions with oddly specific details
jokes.explanation: Michael's characteristic over-the-top escalation with dark humor. The specificity (groin, 100 times) makes it memorable.

jokes.text: They say that 15 Chinese soldiers fought off the entire army of Genghis Khan just using this stuff.
jokes.setup: Creed is looking at Chinese virility herbs
jokes.punchline: Makes up an absurdly specific historical 'fact' about the aphrodisiac
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed absurdism. The specific number (15) and impossible scenario are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Better men than Gabe have tried to be my boss. David got fired. Charles got fired. Jan went crazy. Ed Truck, who I liked, got decapitated.
jokes.setup: Michael lists his former bosses to show Gabe isn't special
jokes.punchline: Escalates through firings to Jan going crazy to Ed Truck's gruesome death, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure building to dark punchline. The casual delivery of 'decapitated' is perfect.

jokes.text: In the Schrute family, the youngest child always raises the others. I've been raising children since I was a baby.
jokes.setup: Dwight explains Schrute family child-rearing traditions while holding Cece
jokes.punchline: Claims he's been raising children since he was a baby himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight absurdism about his bizarre upbringing. The logical impossibility is delivered with total conviction.

jokes.text: And I'd blow your mind.
jokes.setup: Kevin is trying to comfort Andy about Erin choosing Gabe
jokes.punchline: Ends his pep talk with an unexpectedly sexual boast
jokes.explanation: Kevin's inappropriate overshare ruins his attempt at being supportive. Economical but predictable.

jokes.text: Was it worth it, Jim? 'Cause we missed it.
jokes.setup: Jim checked sports scores during Glee causing them to miss a song
jokes.punchline: Erin's passive-aggressive guilt trip delivered with fake sweetness
jokes.explanation: Erin's uncharacteristic passive-aggression. Good character moment but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Just go to Gabe's refrigerator, get a lump of suet, or any kind of congealed animal fat will do, really. Tie a piece of string to it, tie the other end to her toe, pop the suet in her mouth, she'll be happy for hours.
jokes.setup: Dwight offers baby-care advice to Pam
jokes.punchline: Suggests treating the baby like a farm animal with elaborate suet contraption
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight treating humans like livestock. The elaborate specificity makes it memorably absurd.

jokes.text: I just ate an entire seahorse. I have to admit, I did not think it was gonna work, but it's totally working. I feel exactly like a seahorse.
jokes.setup: Kevin consumed Chinese virility herbs containing seahorse
jokes.punchline: Claims to feel like a seahorse and makes neighing sounds
jokes.explanation: Kevin's literal interpretation of feeling like what he ate. Visual element with neighing adds to the joke.

jokes.text: So, these are pizza dogs. These aren't pigs in a blanket, per se.
jokes.setup: Gabe is serving what he calls pigs in a blanket
jokes.punchline: Admits they're actually pizza dough wrapped hot dogs, not real pigs in a blanket
jokes.explanation: Gabe's pretentious excuse for lazy cooking. Mildly cringey but not exceptional.

jokes.text: That's what she said.
jokes.setup: Gabe says 'you are making this harder than it has to be'
jokes.punchline: Michael delivers his signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Michael's signature line. Not original but flawlessly executed and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I've always considered us to be very good friends. Great friends.
jokes.setup: Jim needs Dwight's help with babysitting
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately corrects 'very good' to 'great' friends, showing his desperation for friendship
jokes.explanation: Dwight's eager upgrade of their friendship status reveals his neediness. Good character insight.

jokes.text: Surprise me. No. Pepperoni.
jokes.setup: Jim asks what kind of pizza Dwight wants
jokes.punchline: Dwight starts to say 'surprise me' then immediately takes it back with specific order
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight contradiction - wants to seem adventurous but needs control. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Better than English. (IN MOCK MANDARIN)
jokes.setup: Asked if he reads Chinese
jokes.punchline: Claims he reads it better than English then speaks gibberish
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed confident nonsense. The follow-up gibberish reinforces the absurdity perfectly.

jokes.text: You're four seasons in a day.
jokes.setup: Andy is trying to compliment Erin while clearly drunk/sick
jokes.punchline: Garbled attempt at poetic compliment about her outfit
jokes.explanation: Andy's drunken poetry attempt. Character-appropriate but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Just don't think of it as degrading. Think of it as you happen to be moving the pizza six inches this way, and he happens to be biting it.
jokes.setup: Jim has to feed Dwight pizza while he holds the baby
jokes.punchline: Pam tries to make the humiliating situation sound less degrading with elaborate rationalization
jokes.explanation: Pam's sweet attempt to help Jim maintain dignity in ridiculous situation. Well-crafted rationalization.

jokes.text: She needs to be serviced.
jokes.setup: Dwight explains his obligation to meet Angela
jokes.punchline: Uses mechanical/car terminology to describe sex with his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight dehumanization of romance. Treating Angela like farm equipment is peak character.

jokes.text: She is in heat. She will eat your face off.
jokes.setup: Jim offers to go talk to Angela for Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight describes Angela using animal breeding terminology
jokes.explanation: Dwight treating human sexuality like animal husbandry. The warning about face-eating is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I wanted to eat a pig in a blanket in a blanket.
jokes.setup: Kevin is found wrapped in a blanket eating the 'pigs in a blanket'
jokes.punchline: Creates a literal triple-layer pun with the food, his blanket, and the concept
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay from Kevin creating layers of meaning. The visual element adds to the humor.

jokes.text: The first time we saw each other naked, we didn't even make love. We just stared at each other until we fell asleep. It was magical.
jokes.setup: Phyllis is giving Erin romantic advice about taking things slow
jokes.punchline: Shares disturbing TMI about her and Bob's first intimate encounter
jokes.explanation: Peak Phyllis oversharing. The romantic framing of such a weird scenario is perfect cringe comedy.

jokes.text: He's a weird, little, skeevy guy with no waist.
jokes.setup: Michael explains why he doesn't like Gabe
jokes.punchline: Lists Gabe's physical shortcomings ending with the oddly specific 'no waist'
jokes.explanation: Michael's cruel but accurate physical assessment. The 'no waist' detail is memorably specific.

jokes.text: I hate your roof. I'll raise the roof.
jokes.setup: Michael says 'as long as you live under my roof' during their fake father-daughter fight
jokes.punchline: Erin plays on both meanings of 'roof' and 'raise'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay in the midst of their roleplay argument. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: If you break that girl's heart, I will kill you. It's just a figure of speech. But seriously, if you break that girl's heart, I will literally kill you and your entire family.
jokes.setup: Michael threatens Gabe about hurting Erin
jokes.punchline: Immediately clarifies it's figurative then escalates to literal murder of entire family
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael escalation structure. The immediate contradiction followed by bigger threat is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: How much? I don't know. It's powdered, so... Like, four or five, I don't know.
jokes.setup: Asked how much seahorse he consumed
jokes.punchline: Kevin's inability to understand powder vs whole animals, estimates 'four or five' seahorses
jokes.explanation: Kevin's simple-minded calculation of powder to whole animals. Good callback to earlier joke.

jokes.text: That's so beautiful.
jokes.setup: Gabe plays pretentious ambient music called 'Earthrise on the Moon'
jokes.punchline: Kevin's sincere appreciation of what's likely terrible new age music
jokes.explanation: Kevin's earnest response to pretentious art. Character-appropriate but mild humor.

jokes.text: Are you gonna quiet that baby or do I have to?
jokes.setup: Cece is crying during the Glee viewing
jokes.punchline: Angela threatens to handle someone else's crying baby
jokes.explanation: Angela's controlling nature extends to other people's children. Perfectly in character.

tmdb_overview: Michael sabotages Gabe's party while excluded; Angela requests intimacy from Dwight.
